div.h1: File I/O Benchmark: io_uring vs pread

g.text: Concurrency | pread (MB/s) | io_uring reg | io_uring fixed
Throughput vs Concurrency (4KB reads, NVMe SSD)

div.p: • Test conditions: Linux 6.1, NVMe SSD (Samsung 980 Pro), ext4, direct I/O • Syscall measurement: perf stat -e syscalls:sys_enter_io_uring_enter,syscalls:sys_enter_pread64 • Crossover point: Concurrency ≈ 2-3 is where io_uring overhead amortization exceeds pread • Fixed buffer registration: One-time cost of ~50μs for 256 buffers, negligible for long-running servers • Cache effects: At high concurrency, L3 cache pressure affects all methods similarly
Key Insight: At concurrency=1, all methods perform similarly (syscall per op). At concurrency≥4, io_uring batching dominates. Fixed buffers add ~10% by eliminating per-op validation.
